name: Public Safety Analytics
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: GPT-4o for incident analysis — crime pattern narrative generation, resource allocation reasoning, community sentiment summarization, cross-agency intelligence briefings

services.name: Azure Machine Learning
services.purpose: Predictive models for crime pattern forecasting, resource demand prediction, hotspot identification, temporal trend analysis, and risk scoring

enterprise.note: GPU compute, deep learning models, real-time inference

services.name: Azure Event Hubs
services.purpose: Real-time ingestion of 911 dispatch events, CAD system feeds, sensor alerts, social media signals, traffic camera events, and inter-agency data streams

services.name: Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Incident records, crime patterns, resource deployment history, community sentiment data, officer activity logs, geospatial hotspot data

enterprise.note: Multi-region, analytical store for historical analysis

services.name: Azure Stream Analytics
services.purpose: Real-time event correlation — pattern matching across 911 calls, sensor alerts, and dispatch events; sliding window anomaly detection; geospatial clustering

services.name: Container Apps
services.purpose: Public safety API — incident analysis engine, resource optimizer, community dashboard backend, inter-agency data sharing, reporting engine

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: CJIS-compliant secret management — CAD system credentials, inter-agency API keys, officer identity encryption, evidence chain-of-custody keys

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: System uptime (mission-critical), event processing latency, model prediction accuracy, API availability, alert delivery timing

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini for routine daily incident summaries and shift briefing drafts — reserve GPT-4o for complex multi-source pattern analysis and cross-agency intelligence reports, saving 65% on AI costs
Right-size Stream Analytics streaming units based on actual event volume — most jurisdictions have predictable load patterns with peaks during evening hours; scale down during low-activity periods
Implement tiered retention in Cosmos DB — active incidents in hot storage (90 days), resolved cases archived to analytical store, historical aggregates retained indefinitely for trend analysis
Use Event Hubs capture to Azure Storage for raw event archival instead of keeping extended retention in Event Hubs — blob storage is 90% cheaper than Event Hubs retention

Schedule ML model retraining during weekly maintenance windows rather than daily — crime pattern models benefit from weekly aggregation and retraining more frequently adds cost without accuracy improvement
Set Azure Cost Management budgets with alerts at 50%, 80%, 100%
